ajoute.description: [Traduction en cours] Adds a value to a ~l:variable~ or adds two ~m:values~ and puts the result into a ~m:variable~. See elsewhere in this documentation for an explanation of what is meant by a ~m:value~. If you add to a ~m: variable~ it must already hold a numeric value, but if you assign a ~m:variable~ to hold the result of an addition it will lose whatever value it previously held.
ajoute.examples: ajoute 1 à/a N%0aajoute N à/a Total donnant Total

soustrais/retire.description: [Traduction en cours] Subtracts a value from a variable, or subtracts one value from another and puts the result into a variable.
soustrais/retire.examples: soustrais/retire 5 depuis Count%0asoustrais/retire X depuis Y donnant Z

multiplie.description: [Traduction en cours] Multiplies a variable by a value, or multiplies two values and puts the result into a variable.
multiplie.examples: multiplie X par Y%0amultiplie X par 2 donnant Y

divise.description: [Traduction en cours] Divides a ~m:variable~ by a ~m:value~, or divides one ~m:value~ by another, putting the result into a ~m:variable~.
divise.examples: divise X par 2~lf~divide X par Y donnant Z

efface.description: [Traduction en cours] Assigns the boolean value ~m:false~ to a ~l:variable~, replacing whatever it previously held. See also ~l:set~ and ~l:toggle~.

début/debut.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:begin~ introduces a compound statement; a block of commands that start with ~m:begin~ and finish with ~l:end~. The entire block is treated as a single statement and ~l:end~ marks the end of the compound statement block.

alias.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:alias~ lets you use one variable in place of another. Suppose you have a number of similar objects, having identical structures, where an action must be taken with just one of them, or the same action in turn on all of them. AllSpeak has arrays but in some cases these are already in use and the language does not support multidimensional arrays. In such cases you will often find that using an alias will provide you with an efficient way to perform a standard set of operations on any one of the objects.%0a%0aThe type of the ~m:alias~ variable can be anything, but for clarity it~sq~s best to use a variable of the same type as the one being aliased. To see alias in use, take a look at our [Factory Simulation](resources/ecs/sample/factory). (Copy the script into the editor window and run it.)

module.description: [Traduction en cours] Defines a special kind of variable whose job is to hold information about a script module that has been run from the current script. See also ~l:run~ and ~l:send~.

callback.description: [Traduction en cours] A ~m:callback~ is a special kind of variable that is used to handle events arising from other special variable types such as the ~l:showdown|k|showdown!Showdown~ plugin. See ~l:on!on {callback}~ and ~l:payload|v!payload~.
callback.examples: callback DecoratorCallback~lf~on DecoratorCallback va à/a ProcessEvent

ferme.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:close~ raises an ~m:on close~ event in the called ~l:module~, which is taken to be a request for it to close itself. This typically means removing all screen elements.

debug.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:debug~ is a tool for examining some aspect of your script after it has been compiled. This can be useful when trying to track down obscure spelling mistakes. Anyone wanting to build a plugin to extend the features of AllSpeak will probably need to use ~m:debug~ from time to time.%0a%0aYou can view the entire program using ~m:debug program~.%0a%0aYou can debug the single program item at a given program counter address, using ~m:debug program item {pc}~ or ~m:debug program pc {pc}~.%0a%0aYou can show the entire symbol table using ~m:debug symbols~.%0a%0aYou can look at a single symbol using ~m:debug symbol {name}~.%0a%0aYou can use ~m:debug step~ to cause the running program to output to the console the name of the script (assuming the ~l:script~ command has been given) and the program counter, for every step of the running program.
debug.examples: debug programme%0adebug programme pc 12%0adebug script%0adebug symbole Count%0adebug pas

décode/decode.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:decode~ performs a decoding operation on a text value. The default encoding translates single and double quotes into special character sequences and is mainly for use when storing HTML, JSON or other data in your own database using AllSpeak~sq~s REST server.%0a%0aSee also ~l:set!set the encoding~, which defines the type of encoding to be used by both ~m:decode~ and ~l:encode~.

encode.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:encode~ performs an encoding operation on a text value. The default encoding translates single and double quotes into special character sequences and is mainly for use when storing HTML or other data in your own database using AllSpeak~sq~s REST server.~lf~~lf~See also ~l:set!set the encoding~, which defines the type of encoding to be used by both ~m:encode~ and ~l:decode~.

fin.description: [Traduction en cours] Terminates a block of commands started by ~l:begin~. These blocks behave like single instructions.
fin.examples: mets 0 dans Count%0atant Count est inférieur/inferieur à/a 10%0adébut/debut%0a  wait 1 seconde%0a  add 1 à/a Count%0afin

bifurque.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:fork~ is a special kind of ~l:goto~, that causes the program to execute from the given label but also continue executing at the next command. The multitasking implied in this is handled by _**AllSpeak**_. JavaScript programmers will know that the language is single threaded, so a cooperative technique is used internally to create the illusion of true multitasking.%0a

va.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:go to~ and ~m:goto~ transfer control to the command at the named  ~m:label~. The command(s) following the ~m:go~/~m:goto~ are not executed at that time.

vasous.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:gosub~ is like ~l:go~ in that it transfers control to the named label. However, when the program encounters a ~l:return~ command, execution resumes at the command following the ~m:gosub~.

si.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:if~ tests the condition that follows. If the result is ~m:true~ then control resumes at the named label; otherwise if there~sq~s an ~m:else~ section this is executed, then the program resumes at the next instruction after the ~m:if~. (Unless a ~l:goto~ or equivalent was encountered as in the second example.)
si.examples: si Value1 est supérieur/superieur à/a Value2 mets vrai dans Result%0asi Remaining est 0 arrête/arrete%0asinon va à/a RepeatRepeatRepeatRepeatRepeatRepeat

importe.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:import~ is part of a mechanism by which programs are broken up into separate ~link:module~s that run independently. This lets you a) write smaller programs, b) separate parts of the program that don~sq~t interact much and c) include code downloaded as a plugin.%0a%0aThe ~m:import~ command sets up a list of variables that are required to be exported by the calling ~l:module~. This list must match in both number and type, though you can use different names for the variables on each side.%0a%0aBy exporting variables to another script module you are allowing it to work on them independently of your own code. References made to the variable by the called script are working on the actual variables from the parent script.%0a%0aAs a result it goes without saying that after you import a variable its value may change if the calling script goes on working concurrently with your script. This is a situation that you will usually avoid, but there are times when it is useful to be able to follow changes to a variable supplied by another script.
importe.examples: importe variable Count%0a  and div Items%0a  and bouton/button SortButton

index.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:index~ is a special command for array handling. In AllSpeak, all variables are arrays. Initially they only have one element but you can set an array to have as many elements as you like, using ~l:set!set the elements of {variable} to {value}~. Inside each array is the current index for the array, which you can set to any value within the number of elements in the array. The array is still used as if it were a simple variable and only the element pointed to by the index is affected by whatever you do with the array. Where multitasking is used - with ~l:fork~ or by handling an event - you must be careful to set the index before using the array if there~sq~s any chance it may have been modified by another part of the program.
index.examples: index List à/a 5%0amets Result dans List

oppose.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:negate~ returns the value of the given variable subtracted from zero.

sur.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:on~ defines what happens when an event occurs. The core package currently handles ~m:trigger~, ~m:close~, ~m:restore~, ~m:message~ and ~m:callback~ events; other packages contain their own events and handlers.%0a%0aA ~m:close~ event is a message from a parent to its child script, telling it to finish processing. It may then remain in a pending state, waiting for a ~m:trigger~ to continue, or it may terminate itself completely. This is up to the programmer to decide.%0a%0aA ~m:restore~ event is received by a script when the browser Back button is clicked or a child script gives a ~l:history|k|browser!history back~ command. In either case, the recipient of the event is the script that is ~dq~previous~dq~ in the window state hierarchy. Scripts can do their own window state management without the need to run separate scripts.%0a%0aA ~m:message~ event is generated when a ~l:send!message~ is received by one script from another; either from the parent to the child ~l:module~ or from a ~l:module~ to its parent. See ~l:send~. Messages are a very useful way of exchanging information between modules; they often take the form of JSON structures that contain everything needed for the recipient to handle the message. The content of the message is available as ~l:message|v!the message~.%0a%0aA ~m:callback~ event is generated by certain things like the markdown processor in the ~m:showdown~ package, which uses it to notify its own extensions that they should process some part of the text. See the documentation for that package.

imprime.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:print~ evaluates what follows and writes it to the browser console. Use it for debugging.%0a%0aSee also ~l:browser:alert~%0a%0aIf the text to be printed starts with [ or {~dq~ it is assumed to be JSON-formatted and will be printed using ~m:JSON.stringify(null,2)~ so as to be more readable.
imprime.examples: imprime `The counter est maintenant ` cat Counter

mets.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:put~ evaluates what follows and puts it into the named ~l:variable~, overwriting whatever was previously there. Note the use of ~m:cat~ in the last example, to join together several parts of a string of text.
mets.examples: mets 59 dans Value%0amets Value1 dans Value2%0amets `Some data` dans MyData%0amets `Counting ` cat N cat ` times` dans Message

remplace.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:replace~ searches for all occurrences of a string in an item of text and replaces it with another string.
remplace.examples: remplace `%0a` avec nouvelleligne dans Text%0aremplace Original avec Replacement dans Line

retourne.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:return~ marks the end of a subroutine - see ~l:gosub~. Program execution continues at the command following the ~m:gosub~.

exécute/execute.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:run ~causes a second (or third, fourth...) script module to be compiled and run. The syntax is%0a%0a~m:run {variable} [with {exports}] [as {module}]~%0a%0awhere%0a~m:{variable}~ is a variable containing the full URL of the script file%0a~m:{exports}~ is an optional list of exported variables. These will need to match the list of imports in the loaded script.%0a~m:{module}~ is an optional variable holding the module reference. You~sq~ll need this if you want to send a ~l:send!message~.%0a%0aIf ~m:nowait~ is added to the end of the command, the calling script will not wait for a ~l:set!set ready~ to be given before continuing.%0a%0aIf ~m:then {block}~ is added, the block of instructions will be executed when the script is aborted or reaches an ~l:exit~ command.
exécute/execute.examples: exécute/execute Ticker%0aexécute/execute Helper comme HelperModule sansattente%0aexécute/execute Editor avec variable MainText et variable Index et div Panel comme EditModule%0aexécute/execute MyScript puis alerte `MyScript ended`

script.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:script~ lets you name the module; useful when debugging multiple scripts.

envoie.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:send~ lets you send a message to a module created using ~l:run~. The message will often be JSON-formatted and will contain complete information about what is being requested. See also ~l:on!on message~.%0a%0aThe form ~m:send {message} to parent~ sends a message to the module that launched this one.%0a%0aIf the ~m:{message}~ is omitted then a message is sent to the recipient but with no data attached.
envoie.examples: envoie Message à/a MyModule%0aenvoie Message à/a parent%0aenvoie à/a MyModule%0aenvoie à/a parent

définis/definis.syntax: définis/definis {variable}%0adéfinis/definis {variable} à/a {value}%0adéfinis/definis {variable} à/a tableau/objet%0adéfinis/definis {variable} à/a {value} [{value}...]%0adéfinis/definis le/la/les/l éléments/elements de {variable} à/a {value}%0adéfinis/definis le/la/les/l encoding à/a url/ec/assainis%0adéfinis/definis élément/element/propriété/propriete {name} de {variable} à/a {value}%0adéfinis/definis le/la/les/l payload de {callback} à/a Data%0adéfinis/definis prêt/pret
définis/definis.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:set~ is probably the most heavily used command in _**AllSpeak**_. You~sq~ll find variations of it in most of the plugin modules. Here in the core package it does all the following, as listed in the **Syntax** above:%0a%0a-- assigns the boolean true value to the named variable. See also ~l:clear~ and ~l:toggle~.  %0a%0a-- assigns a value to a variable (same as ~l:put!put {value} into {variable}~).%0a%0a-- initializes a variable to be either an empty array or an empty object%0a%0a-- assigns an array of constant data to a variable. %0a %0a-- converts a variable to an array, assigning the number of elements. When used to change the size of an array the function preserves all elements that are not affected by the size change.%0a%0a-- sets the encoding/decoding method. The default is ~m:ec~, which encodes single and double quote characters and line breaks; you can also have ~m:url~, which performs standard URL-encoding and decoding, and ~m:sanitize~, which converts accented characters to their normal (unaccented) equivalents (this one has no decoding equivalent). Once set, the encoding method is kept for the session or until altered again with another ~m:set~.%0a%0a-- Sets an element or a property of a variable, converting it to a JSON string if it was previously empty. %0a %0a-- Sets the ~m:payload~ value in a ~l:callback~ variable. %0a %0a-- Set the state of a newly-~l:run~ module to ~m:ready~, which frees the parent script to continue running.
définis/definis.examples: définis/definis Flag%0adéfinis/definis Value à/a 59%0adéfinis/definis Map à/a objet%0adéfinis/definis Names à/a tableau%0adéfinis/definis Squares à/a 1 4 9 16 25 36 49 64 81 100%0adéfinis/definis le/la/les/l éléments/elements de ThisVariable à/a 10%0adéfinis/definis le/la/les/l encoding à/a `url`%0adéfinis/definis élément/element 5 de Item à/a NewValue%0adéfinis/definis propriété/propriete `name` de Item à/a `first`%0adéfinis/definis le/la/les/l payload de Callback à/a MyData%0adéfinis/definis prêt/pret

arrête/arrete.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:stop~ causes the current program execution thread to halt. Other threads may continue and the program will still respond to events.%0a%0aBy ~dq~threads~dq~ we mean the pseudo-threads _**AllSpeak**_ uses to simulate multi-tasking. These operate on a cooperative basis, handing control from one to the next when a command such as ~l:wait~ is encountered. This enables, for example, animations to be created where several things are happening at once, such as in our [Factory Simulation](https://allspeak.software/factory/).

bascule.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:toggle~ converts the boolean value held in a ~l:variable~ from ~m:true~ to ~m:false~ or vice versa. See also ~l:set~ and ~l:clear~.

variable.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:variable~ defines a variable, that can hold a numeric, boolean or string value. Initially it contains none of these; a value must be assigned (e.g. using ~l:put~) before the variable can be used. If a variable is defined to be an array with more than one element (see ~l:set~ ~m:the elements~), each element can hold a different type (numeric, boolean or string).

attends.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:wait~ causes program execution to stop for a given time, expressed in ~m:millis~, ~m: ticks~, ~m:seconds~ or ~m:minutes~`. A ~m:milli~ is a millisecond and a ~m:tick~ is 10 milliseconds. The default is ~m:seconds~. Only the current program thread stops; others may continue and events will still be handled.~lf~~lf~By ~dq~thread~dq~ we mean the pseudo-threads _**AllSpeak**_ uses to simulate multi-tasking. These operate on a cooperative basis, handing control from one to the next when a command such as ~l:wait~ is encountered. In practice, in a well-designed browser application threads are short enough for this task switching to be relatively rare.

tant.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:while~ tests a condition, and if the result of the test is true it executes the command (or ~m:begin...end~ block) that follows. It then repeats the test and continues to do so until the test fails.%0a%0aWhen constructing loops like this it~sq~s common for programmers to forget to bump the loop counter, resulting in a tight loop that can bring the browser - and the computer - to its knees and risk overheating of the CPU in the process. It~sq~s surprisingly easy to get this wrong during development! _**AllSpeak**_ looks out for this happening and usually stops your script before any harm can be done.
tant.examples: mets 0 dans N%0atant N est inférieur/inferieur à/a 10%0adébut/debut%0a  gosub DoSomething%0a  add 1 à/a N%0afin

requiert.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:require~ loads a CSS or JavaScript file from a remote URL into the HEAD of your document, upon which it will be immediately ready for use. A typical use is for a weather widget.

assainis.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:sanitize~ deals with a problem when JSON data is embedded in HTML as preformatted data and gains unwanted characters such as line breaks. This command removes the unwanted characters and replaces the variable with cleaned-up data.

quitte.description: [Traduction en cours] Causes the script to stop and release all its data.

accole.description: [Traduction en cours] Appends an item to a JSON array, which is is a string value held in a single variable element (not to be confused with a multi-element variable). See also ~l:sort~ and ~l:element|v~.
accole.examples: mets vide dans ItemArray%0aaccole `First value` à/a ItemArray%0aaccole `Second value` à/a ItemArray

trie.description: [Traduction en cours] This is a powerful generalized sort function that will sort any JSON array using a function supplied in the script. In the following description, refer to the example above.~lf~~lf~The sort function is called several times, each with a pair of elements to compare. These are always called ~m:a~ and ~m:b~ and are supplied as named arguments in the array variable. The function does whatever is needed in the context and places the result value (1, 0 or -1) into a ~m:v~ argument.~lf~~lf~Because the variable is a JSON string you can perform comparisons of any complexity by diving into the elements supplied. So you can do the sort on a property of the elements rather than the elements themselves.~lf~~lf~See also ~l:filter~ and ~l:append~.
trie.examples: variable Items~lf~  variable Arg1~lf~  variable Arg2~lf~  variable Result~lf~    ~lf~  put vide dans Items~lf~  append `fish` à/a Items~lf~  append `cabbage` à/a Items~lf~  append `sugar` à/a Items~lf~  append `beef` à/a Items~lf~  append `potatoes` à/a Items~lf~  sort Items avec ArraySorter~lf~  alert Items~lf~  exit~lf~~lf~ArraySorter:~lf~  put arg `a` de Items dans Arg1~lf~  put arg `b` de Items dans Arg2~lf~  if Arg1 est supérieur/superieur à/a Arg2 mets 1 dans Result~lf~  else si Arg1 est inférieur/inferieur à/a Arg2 mets -1 dans Result~lf~  else mets 0 dans Result~lf~  set arg `v` de Items à/a Result~lf~  stop

filtre.description: [Traduction en cours] This is a generalized filter function that will extract wanted values from a JSON array using a function supplied in the script. In the following description, refer to the example above.~lf~~lf~The filter function is called once for every element in the array, each with the element. This is always called ~m:a~ and is supplied as a named argument in the array variable. The function does whatever is needed in the context and places the result value (true or false) into a ~m:v~ argument.~lf~~lf~See also ~l:sort~ and ~l:append~.
filtre.examples: variable Items~lf~  variable A~lf~  variable V~lf~    ~lf~  put vide dans Items~lf~  append `fish` à/a Items~lf~  append `cabbage` à/a Items~lf~  append `sugar` à/a Items~lf~  append `beef` à/a Items~lf~  append `potatoes` à/a Items~lf~  filter Items avec MyFilter~lf~  alert Items~lf~  exit~lf~~lf~MyFilter:~lf~  put arg `a` de Items dans A~lf~  if le/la/les/l longueur de A est supérieur/superieur à/a 5~lf~    set V~lf~    else efface V~lf~  set arg `v` de Items à/a V~lf~  stop

CORE.description: [Traduction en cours] The ~m:core~ package contains all the basic stuff required of any programming language: variables, control structures, values and conditionals. With these it~sq~s possible to write and run simple command-line programs.nnThe other packages all provide features related to specific areas of programming, such as for browsers, storage, comms and special things like maps and text editors.

factice.description: [Traduction en cours] This is a dummy keyword that makes a useful place to set a breakpoint when debugging at JavaScript level, for instance when writing new plugin code.

chaque.description: [Traduction en cours] This sets up an endlessly repeating timer that ~lf~performs the same action over and over again at the specified interval.
chaque.examples: chaque 5 minutes~lf~begin~lf~  print `5 minutes`~lf~  add 1 à/a RepeatCount~lf~end~lf~every 1 seconde vasous à/a DoSomething

scinde.description: [Traduction en cours] This splits a string into an array of elements in a named variable, overwriting anything previously held by that variable. By default the split is done on lines.
scinde.examples: scinde Document dans Lines~lf~split Sentence sur ` ` dans Words

assert.description: [Traduction en cours] Assert that a condition is true; raises an error if the condition fails.
assert.examples: assert X est supérieur/superieur à/a 0 avec `X must be positive`

copie.description: [Traduction en cours] Copy a ~l:variable~, ~l:dictionary~ or ~l:list~ to another of the same type.

crée/cree.description: [Traduction en cours] Create a directory at the given path.

decrement.description: [Traduction en cours] Subtract 1 from an integer ~l:variable~. See also ~l:increment~, ~l:add~ and ~l:take~.

supprime.description: [Traduction en cours] Delete a file, or remove an entry/item/property/element from a ~l:variable~, ~l:dictionary~ or ~l:list~.

dictionary.description: [Traduction en cours] Declare a dictionary ~l:variable~. See also ~l:entry|v~ and ~l:keys|v~.

download.description: [Traduction en cours] Download a file from a URL and save it to a local path.

fichier/file.description: [Traduction en cours] Declare a file ~l:variable~. Use with ~l:open~, ~l:read~, ~l:write~ and ~l:close~.

obtiens.description: [Traduction en cours] Perform an HTTP GET request and store the result in a ~l:variable~.

goto.description: [Traduction en cours] Jump to a labelled location in the script. See also ~l:go~ and ~l:gosub~.

increment.description: [Traduction en cours] Add 1 to an integer ~l:variable~. See also ~l:decrement~, ~l:add~ and ~l:take~.

input.description: [Traduction en cours] Read a line of input from the terminal into a ~l:variable~.

list.description: [Traduction en cours] Declare a list ~l:variable~. See also ~l:append~, ~l:item|v~ and ~l:count|v~.

charge.description: [Traduction en cours] Load text content from a file into a ~l:variable~. See also ~l:save~.

lock.description: [Traduction en cours] Lock a ~l:variable~ to prevent modification. See also ~l:unlock~.

journalise.description: [Traduction en cours] Print a value to the console with a timestamp and script name prefix. See also ~l:print~.

open.description: [Traduction en cours] Open a ~l:file~ for reading, writing or appending. See also ~l:read~, ~l:write~ and ~l:close~.

pass.description: [Traduction en cours] A no-op command; does nothing. Useful as a debugger breakpoint.

dépile/depile.description: [Traduction en cours] Pop a value from a ~l:stack~ or ~l:queue~ into a ~l:variable~. See also ~l:push~.

poste.description: [Traduction en cours] Perform an HTTP POST request, sending content to a URL.

empile.description: [Traduction en cours] Push a value onto a ~l:stack~ or ~l:queue~. See also ~l:pop~.

queue.description: [Traduction en cours] Declare a queue (FIFO) ~l:variable~. See also ~l:push~ and ~l:pop~.

read.description: [Traduction en cours] Read content from an open ~l:file~ into a ~l:variable~. See also ~l:open~ and ~l:write~.

release.description: [Traduction en cours] Release the parent script so it can continue execution after a ~l:module~ call.

reset.description: [Traduction en cours] Reset a ~l:variable~ to its initial (empty) state.

save.description: [Traduction en cours] Save content to a local file. See also ~l:load~.

mélange/melange.description: [Traduction en cours] Randomly shuffle the elements of a ~l:list~ ~l:variable~.

ssh.description: [Traduction en cours] Declare an SSH connection ~l:variable~ for remote file operations.

stack.description: [Traduction en cours] Declare a stack (LIFO) ~l:variable~. See also ~l:push~ and ~l:pop~.

system.description: [Traduction en cours] Execute a system shell command. See also ~l:system|v~ for capturing output.

rogne.description: [Traduction en cours] Remove leading and trailing whitespace from a ~l:variable~. See also ~l:trim|v~.

truncate.description: [Traduction en cours] Truncate an open ~l:file~ to zero length.

use.description: [Traduction en cours] Load a plugin or enable a built-in module (e.g. ~m:use server~).

write.description: [Traduction en cours] Write content to an open ~l:file~. See also ~l:open~ and ~l:read~.

test.description: [Traduction en cours] Run a test assertion. Used for internal testing.

langage.description: [Traduction en cours] Declares the human language used by the script. This must appear before any other code. The ~m:{name}~ can be an ISO language code (e.g. ~m:it~, ~m:en~) or a language label (e.g. ~m:italiano~, ~m:english~). The compiler loads the matching language pack and uses it to interpret all subsequent keywords.%0a%0aIn the JS version the pack is looked up first by direct name, then by scanning loaded packs for a matching ~m:meta.label~ or ~m:meta.language~. The Python version behaves identically: it first tries a direct filename match, then scans all language pack files for a matching label.

info.description: [Traduction en cours] A metadata directive that embeds descriptive text in a script. The compiler skips this command entirely; it is only used by the CLI ~m:allspeak info {script}~ command to extract and display information about a script.%0a%0aThe ~m:{text}~ argument uses the same backtick-delimited literal syntax as other string values, so it can span multiple lines. If the text is valid JSON it will be pretty-printed when displayed.%0a%0aFrom the command line:%0a~m:allspeak info~ — lists all ~m:.as~ files in the current directory.%0a~m:allspeak info {name}~ — displays the info text from the named script (the ~m:.as~ extension is optional).
info.examples: info `This script demonstrates basic Italian AllSpeak.%0aIt counts depuis 0 à/a 4 et prints a greeting.`

vrai.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the boolean value ~m:true~. This is not often needed as it~sq~s usually implied from the context.

faux.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the boolean value ~m:false~. This is not often needed as it's usually implied from the context.

vide.examples: si Text est vide mets `default` dans Text

nouvelleligne.description: [Traduction en cours] A new line character.
nouvelleligne.examples: imprime Line1 cat nouvelleligne cat Line2

maintenant.description: [Traduction en cours] The timestamp - the number of seconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970).%0a%0aSee also ~l:today~, ~l:date~ and ~l:format~.

modulo.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns one integer ~m:value~ modulo another, which is to say, the remainder after dividing the former by the latter.

propriété/propriete.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:property~ is used to access an item in a JSON structure. In _**AllSpeak**_, JSON objects are ~dq~stringified~dq~ and kept in plain text variables. When you request a ~m:property~ from such an object it is first parsed then the requested property is extracted for you. See also ~l:element~ and ~l:set|k!set property~.
propriété/propriete.examples: mets propriété/propriete `name` de PersonalData dans Name~lf~put propriété/propriete ID de Record dans ID

élément/element.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:element~ is used to access an item in a JSON array. In _**AllSpeak**_, JSON objects are ~dq~stringified~dq~ and kept in plain text variables. When you request an ~m:element~ from such an object it is first parsed then the requested element is extracted for you. See also ~l:property~.
élément/element.examples: mets élément/element 5 de MyArray dans Value~lf~put élément/element N de Data dans Item

aléatoire/aleatoire.description: [Traduction en cours] ~m:random~ returns a random number between 0 and the ~m:value~ given.
aléatoire/aleatoire.examples: ajoute aléatoire/aleatoire 10 à/a Count%0asoustrais/retire aléatoire/aleatoire Size depuis Value donnant V

cos.description: [Traduction en cours] returns the cosine of the angle given, multiplied by the specified radius. See also ~l:sin~ and ~l:tan~.
cos.examples: mets cos X rayon R dans Length

sin.description: [Traduction en cours] returns the sine of the angle given, multiplied by the specified radius. See also ~l:cos~ and ~l:tan~.%0a
sin.examples: mets sin X rayon R dans Length

tan.description: [Traduction en cours] returns the tangent of the angle given, multiplied by the specified radius. See also ~l:sin~ and ~l:cos~.
tan.examples: mets tan X rayon R dans Length

gauche.description: [Traduction en cours] returns the leftmost ~m:{count}~ characters of ~m:{text}~.%0a
gauche.examples: mets gauche 5 de Text dans Prefix

droite.description: [Traduction en cours] returns the rightmost ~m:{count}~ characters of ~m:{Text}~.
droite.examples: mets droite 5 de Text dans Suffix

depuis.description: [Traduction en cours] Extracts part of a string, given the start and end character indices. The characters taken start at the first index and continue to one before the end index. If no end index is given, everything up to the end of the string is taken.
depuis.examples: mets depuis 8 de Sentence dans Phrase~lf~put depuis X à/a Y de `the quick brown fox` dans Extract

position.description: [Traduction en cours] returns the position in ~m:{haystack}~ of either the first or the last occurrence of ~m:{needle}~, where both ~m:{needle}~ and ~m:{haystack}~ are text values.%0a%0aIf ~m:{needle}~ is not contained in ~m:{haystack}~ the value ~m:-1~ is returned.%0a%0aIf the optional ~m:nocase~ is given the match is done with case ignored.%0a
position.examples: mets le/la/les/l position de `fox` dans `The quick brown fox jumps over le/la/les/l lazy dog` dans Pos (Pos=16)%0amets le/la/les/l position nocase de le/la/les/l dernier `dot` dans `Dot dot Dot` dans P (P=8)

décode/decode.description: [Traduction en cours] decodes the ~m:variable~ using the current encoding (see ~l:set|k~). See also ~l:encode~.

encode.description: [Traduction en cours] decodes the ~m:variable~ using the current encoding (see ~l:set|k~). See also ~l:decode~.%0a

minuscule.description: [Traduction en cours] Converts the value to lower case; useful for string comparisons.%0a

index.syntax: le/la/les/l index de {variable}~lf~the index de {value} dans {list}
index.description: [Traduction en cours] The first form gets the current index of ~m:{variable}~. All variables in AllSpeak are arrays, though most only have a single element. Where they have more than one (as set by ~l:set|k!set the elements of {variable}~) the index always points to the current element and the variable acts as if only has that one element.~lf~~lf~The second form gets the index of an element in an array (list).
index.examples: mets le/la/les/l index de Values dans M~lf~put le/la/les/l index de `March` dans Months dans Index

valeur.description: [Traduction en cours] Gets the numeric value of ~m:{value}~.
valeur.examples: mets le/la/les/l valeur de `459` dans N459%0amets le/la/les/l valeur de ThisString dans Code

message.description: [Traduction en cours] This holds the value of the last message received by this module. There is no information about who sent it; if you need this you must put some appropriate content into the message itself. See ~l:send|k~ and ~l:on|k!on message~.

format.description: [Traduction en cours] This ~sq~stringifies~sq~ a numeric value in potentially many different ways. Only one is implemented at present, this being to format dates.%0a%0aThe ~m:{value}~ is a numeric timestamp such as that obtained using ~l:now~. The ~m:{specification}~ is a variable, formatted as in the example above, where ~m:mode~ is the formatting mode, ~m:date~ signifying that date formatting should be done, while ~m:locale~ and ~m:options~ are the parameters required for the JavaScript [Date.toLocaleDateString()](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Date/toLocaleDateString) function. The string output in this case is in this format:%0a%0a`April 21, 2020`
format.examples: définis/definis DateFormat à/a objet%0adéfinis/definis propriété/propriete `mode` de DateFormat à/a `date`%0adéfinis/definis propriété/propriete `locale` de DateFormat à/a `en-EN`%0adéfinis/definis Item à/a objet%0adéfinis/definis propriété/propriete `year` de Item à/a `numeric`%0adéfinis/definis propriété/propriete `month` de Item à/a `long`%0adéfinis/definis propriété/propriete `day` de Item à/a `numeric`%0adéfinis/definis propriété/propriete `options` de DateFormat à/a Item%0amets maintenant dans Timestamp%0aimprime Timestamp format DateFormat

aujourdhui.description: [Traduction en cours] The timestamp - the number of seconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970) up to midnight (the start of) today. See ~l:format~ for an example of usage.~lf~~lf~See also ~l:now~, ~l:date~, ~l:time~ and ~l:format~.

date.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the timestamp of a given date - the number of seconds since the epoch (January 1, 1970).~lf~~lf~The value returned will depend on the format given. The first example above returns 1561420800000, which is the UTC value for the date given, but the second and third return values for the local timezone. The last one returns GMT (which is the same as UTC).~lf~~lf~See also ~l:today~, ~l:now~ and ~l:format~.
date.examples: mets date `2019-06-25` dans TheDate~lf~put date `2019-06-25 00:00:00` dans TheDate~lf~put date `Tuesday June 25 2019` dans TheDate~lf~put date `Tuesday June 25 2019 GMT` dans TheDate

char[acter].description: [Traduction en cours] Extracts a single character from a string. Both ~m:index~ and ~m:value~ can be either symbols or values.
char[acter].examples: mets car N de Text dans C%0aimprime caractère/caractere 5 de ~dq~helpful~dq~ --> ~dq~u~dq~

scinde.description: [Traduction en cours] Splits a value into an array of parts on the given separator. If no separator is given the separator is set to a newline.%0a%0aThe result is an array variable.
scinde.examples: scinde Text dans Lines%0ascinde CSV sur `,` donnant Items%0ascinde `1,2,3,4,5` sur Comma dans Numbers

index de.description: [Traduction en cours] The (numeric) value of the current index of a named array variable. This is the value set by ~l:index!index {variable} to ...~.
index de.examples: mets le/la/les/l index de Button dans Index

longueur de.description: [Traduction en cours] Measures a text variable.
longueur de.examples: mets le/la/les/l longueur de Name dans Length

uuid.description: [Traduction en cours] Generates a UUID - a random string with a very low probability of being repeated. This is usually enough to ensure global uniqueness for most projects.~lf~~lf~In some cases it may be easier to use a value composed of some local feature plus a timestamp, for example~lf~~lf~~m:MyEmailAddress cat now~~lf~~lf~which combines the user~sq~s email address with the current time, guaranteeing a new unique value every second (assuming only one person has the use of that email address). If the rules are kept to, these values are globally unique.

inverse.description: [Traduction en cours] Reverses the given string.
inverse.examples: inverse `The quick brown fox jumps over le/la/les/l lazy dog`

backtick.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the backtick (`) character.

break.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns an HTML break sequence (</br />)

hash.description: [Traduction en cours] Generates a hash of the supplied text.
hash.examples: mets hash `The quick brown fox` dans MyHash

error.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the most recent error message from the program.

arg.description: [Traduction en cours] This is a fairly obscure command that extracts the given property of a variable, returning it as numeric if it can. (See the JavaScript source of the code.js module if further clarification is needed.)
arg.examples: mets arg `count` de Value dans MyArg

payload.description: [Traduction en cours] Retries the payload of a callback variable. See ~l:callback|k!callback~.
payload.examples: mets le/la/les/l payload de MyCallback dans Payload

milliseconde.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current timestamp as the number of milliseconds since January 1st, 1970.

temps.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the number of seconds since midnight last.

année/annee.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current year, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
année/annee.examples: mets le/la/les/l année/annee dans Year~lf~put le/la/les/l année/annee de Timestamp dans Year

heure.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current hour, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
heure.examples: mets le/la/les/l heure dans Hour~lf~put le/la/les/l heure de Timestamp dans Hour

minute.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current minute, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
minute.examples: mets le/la/les/l minute dans Minute~lf~put le/la/les/l minute de Timestamp dans Minute~lf~

seconde.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current second, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
seconde.examples: mets le/la/les/l seconde dans Second~lf~put le/la/les/l seconde de Timestamp dans Second

jour nombre.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current date (day number), either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
jour nombre.examples: mets le/la/les/l jour nombre dans DayNumber~lf~put le/la/les/l jour nombre de Timestamp dans DayNumber

mois nombre.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the current month number, either by default from the current time or from a supplied timestamp.
mois nombre.examples: mets le/la/les/l mois nombre dans MonthNumber~lf~put le/la/les/l mois nombre de Timestamp dans MonthNumber

args.description: [Traduction en cours] The command-line arguments as a JSON list. See also ~l:arg~.

bool.description: [Traduction en cours] Convert a value to a boolean. See also ~l:boolean|v~.

boolean.description: [Traduction en cours] Convert a value to a boolean (alias for ~l:bool|v~).

compte.description: [Traduction en cours] The number of elements in a ~l:list|k~, ~l:dictionary|k~ or multi-element ~l:variable|k~.
compte.examples: mets le/la/les/l compte de MyList dans N

cwd.description: [Traduction en cours] The current working directory path.

datime.description: [Traduction en cours] Format a timestamp using a strftime format string. See also ~l:now~, ~l:today~ and ~l:timestamp~.

éléments/elements.description: [Traduction en cours] The number of elements in a ~l:variable|k~. See also ~l:count|v~.
éléments/elements.examples: mets le/la/les/l éléments/elements de Record dans N

entry.description: [Traduction en cours] Get the value of an entry in a ~l:dictionary|k~ by key.
entry.examples: mets entry `name` de MyDict dans Name

files.description: [Traduction en cours] A JSON list of filenames in a directory. Optionally filtered by ~m:type~.
files.examples: mets le/la/les/l files de `./data` dans FileList

float.description: [Traduction en cours] Convert a value to a floating-point number. See also ~l:integer|v~.

int.description: [Traduction en cours] Convert a value to an integer (alias for ~l:integer|v~).

integer.description: [Traduction en cours] Convert a value to an integer. See also ~l:float|v~.

article.description: [Traduction en cours] Get an item from a ~l:list|k~ by index. See also ~l:index|k~.
article.examples: mets article 0 de MyList dans First

items.description: [Traduction en cours] The items (key-value pairs) of a ~l:dictionary|k~ as JSON.
items.examples: mets le/la/les/l items de MyDict dans AllItems

json.description: [Traduction en cours] Parse a JSON string into a structured value. See also ~l:stringify|v~.

clés/cles.description: [Traduction en cours] The keys of a ~l:dictionary|k~ as a JSON list.
clés/cles.examples: mets le/la/les/l clés/cles de MyDict dans KeyList

modification.description: [Traduction en cours] The last modification timestamp of a file.
modification.examples: mets le/la/les/l modification date de `data.txt` dans ModDate

prettify.description: [Traduction en cours] Format a JSON value with indentation for readability. See also ~l:stringify|v~.

sender.description: [Traduction en cours] The name of the script that sent the most recent inter-module message. See also ~l:send|k~ and ~l:message~.

str.description: [Traduction en cours] Convert a value to a string.

stringify.description: [Traduction en cours] Convert a structured value to a compact JSON string. See also ~l:prettify|v~ and ~l:json|v~.

symbole.description: [Traduction en cours] Get the value of a ~l:variable|k~ by its name as a string.

system.description: [Traduction en cours] Execute a system command and return its output. See also ~l:system|k~.

tab.description: [Traduction en cours] A tab character. See also ~l:newline~.

ticker.description: [Traduction en cours] A monotonically increasing tick counter.

horodatage.description: [Traduction en cours] The current Unix timestamp. See also ~l:now~ and ~l:datime|v~.

rogne.description: [Traduction en cours] A copy of a value with leading and trailing whitespace removed. See also ~l:trim|k~.

type.description: [Traduction en cours] The type name of a value (e.g. ~m:str~, ~m:int~, ~m:dict~, ~m:list~).
type.examples: mets le/la/les/l type de MyVar dans TypeName

uppercase.description: [Traduction en cours] Convert a string to uppercase. See also ~l:lowercase~.

variable.description: [Traduction en cours] Get the raw variable record by name.

weekday.description: [Traduction en cours] The current day of the week as a string (e.g. ~m:Monday~).

acos.description: [Traduction en cours] The arc cosine of a value. See also ~l:cos~, ~l:asin~ and ~l:atan~.
acos.examples: mets acos DY DX dans Angle

asin.description: [Traduction en cours] The arc sine of a value. See also ~l:sin~, ~l:acos~ and ~l:atan~.
asin.examples: mets asin DY DX dans Angle

atan.description: [Traduction en cours] The arc tangent of a value. See also ~l:tan~, ~l:acos~ and ~l:asin~.
atan.examples: mets atan DY DX dans Angle

jour.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the day of the week (0-6). Optionally takes a Unix timestamp.

mois.description: [Traduction en cours] Returns the month (0-11). Optionally takes a Unix timestamp.

module exécution/execution.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a ~l:module|k~ is (or is not) running.
module exécution/execution.examples: si EditorModule est pas exécution/execution goto StartEditor

numérique/numerique.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a value is numeric
numérique/numerique.examples: si TheValue est numérique/numerique ...%0asi car N de Value est pas numérique/numerique ...

pair.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a numerical value is even

impair.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a numerical value is odd

supérieur/superieur.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if one greater is greater than another.
supérieur/superieur.examples: si Count est supérieur/superieur à/a 5 stop~lf~If X est supérieur/superieur à/a Y ...

inférieur/inferieur.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if one value is less than another value.
inférieur/inferieur.examples: si Count est inférieur/inferieur à/a 5 arrête/arrete%0aIf X est inférieur/inferieur à/a Y ...

contient.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a JSON list includes a specific value.

est.syntax: est {value}~lf~is un/une tableau / est un/une objet
est.description: [Traduction en cours] The first form tests if the value given is a specific numeric or string value.~lf~~lf~The second form tests if the given variable holds a JSON array or a JSON object.
est.examples: si Number est 5 {do something}~lf~if Word est `help` va à/a DoHelp~lf~if Variable est un/une tableau DoThis~lf~if Variable est un/une objet DoThat

boolean.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a value is a boolean. See also ~l:numeric~ and ~l:string|c~.

debugging.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if the script is running in debug mode.

vide.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a ~l:variable|k~ is empty (has no value or is an empty string).

existe.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a file exists at the given path.

hasEntry.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a ~l:dictionary|k~ has an entry with the given key. See also ~l:hasProperty|c~.

hasProperty.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a value has a given property. See also ~l:hasEntry|c~.

list.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a value is a list.

none.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a value is ~m:None~.

commence.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a string starts with a given prefix. See also ~l:ends|c~ and ~l:includes~.

termine.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a string ends with a given suffix. See also ~l:starts|c~ and ~l:includes~.

string.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a value is a string. See also ~l:numeric~ and ~l:boolean|c~.

sshError.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if the last ~l:ssh|k~ operation resulted in an error.

sshExists.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a file exists on a remote ~l:ssh|k~ host.

commence avec.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a string starts with a given prefix. See also ~l:ends with|c~ and ~l:includes~.

termine avec.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a string ends with a given suffix. See also ~l:starts with|c~ and ~l:includes~.

tableau.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a value is an array. See also ~l:object|c~.

objet.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if a value is an object. See also ~l:array|c~.

traçage/tracage.description: [Traduction en cours] Tests if the tracer is currently active.
